pir: Will the tracked competitor make a material pricing change within 30 days, and if so in which direction and on which product line?

ground_truth.state: Cirrus has quietly finalized a cut to its Starter plan, set to launch within days, but nothing is public yet and the decision is held closely. The surface picture looks calm and stable: the public pricing page has not moved in over a year and analysts still describe Cirrus pricing as stable. The only indication of the imminent cut is a single, consistently accurate, well-placed source. The correct read is that a high-reliability source reporting something surprising and uncorroborated must be escalated and weighted, not buried for lacking corroboration. The material action in the window is a price cut on the Starter line.

signal_stream.content: A consistently accurate, well-placed former-employee contact reports that Cirrus has quietly finalized a Starter price cut to launch within days, although nothing has been made public.
The public Cirrus pricing page has not changed in over fourteen months.
A market analyst note describes Cirrus pricing as stable and competitive, with no expected near-term moves.
Cirrus published a routine product-update blog post announcing a new reporting dashboard.
A Glassdoor review mentions a routine quarterly all-hands meeting at Cirrus.
Cirrus announced a routine technology partnership with a monitoring vendor.
